milestones.headline: FREEDOM AT MIDNIGHT: INDIA IS BORN
milestones.blurb: At the stroke of midnight on 15 August 1947, India awoke to independence as Nehru delivered his 'Tryst with Destiny' speech and the tricolour rose over Delhi.

milestones.headline: FREE INDIA'S FIRST GOLD AT WEMBLEY
milestones.blurb: On 12 August 1948, India's hockey team beat Great Britain 4-0 at Wembley — the first Olympic gold under the tricolour, won in the land of its former rulers.

milestones.headline: WE, THE PEOPLE, ADOPT A CONSTITUTION
milestones.blurb: On 26 November 1949, the Constituent Assembly adopted the Constitution of India — the world's longest, hand-crafted over nearly three years under Dr. B.R. Ambedkar.

milestones.headline: INDIA BECOMES A REPUBLIC
milestones.blurb: On 26 January 1950, the Constitution came into force and Dr. Rajendra Prasad was sworn in as first President — the world's largest democracy was officially born.

milestones.headline: NEW DELHI HOSTS FIRST-EVER ASIAN GAMES
milestones.blurb: On 4 March 1951, the inaugural Asian Games opened in New Delhi with India finishing second overall. That same year, IIT Kharagpur opened its doors as the first IIT.

milestones.headline: WORLD'S BIGGEST ELECTION CROWNS INDIAN DEMOCRACY
milestones.blurb: Over 173 million voters, most voting for the first time in history, completed the world's largest democratic exercise; the first Lok Sabha convened on 13 May 1952.

milestones.headline: TENZING STANDS ON TOP OF THE WORLD
milestones.blurb: On 29 May 1953, Tenzing Norgay of Darjeeling and Edmund Hillary became the first humans atop Everest. That August, Air India became the nation's flag carrier.

milestones.headline: BHARAT RATNA HONOURS ITS FIRST HEROES
milestones.blurb: India instituted its highest civilian honour in 1954, first celebrating C. Rajagopalachari, S. Radhakrishnan and C.V. Raman; the mighty Bhakra-Nangal canals opened on 8 July.

milestones.headline: STATE BANK OF INDIA OPENS FOR BUSINESS
milestones.blurb: On 1 July 1955, the Imperial Bank was reborn as the State Bank of India, taking banking out of the cities and into villages across the young republic.

milestones.headline: NEW MAP OF INDIA: STATES REDRAWN
milestones.blurb: On 1 November 1956, the States Reorganisation Act redrew India along linguistic lines, creating 14 states and giving millions a state that spoke their language.

milestones.headline: GOODBYE ANNAS, HELLO NAYA PAISA
milestones.blurb: On 1 April 1957, India switched to decimal coinage — 100 naye paise to the rupee. That October, 'Mother India' hit screens and marched to an Oscar nomination.

milestones.headline: FLYING SIKH SPRINTS TO CARDIFF GOLD
milestones.blurb: At the 1958 Cardiff Commonwealth Games, Milkha Singh won the 440 yards in 45.9 seconds — independent India's first athletics gold, and Nehru declared a national holiday.

milestones.headline: TELEVISION ARRIVES: DOORDARSHAN SIGNS ON
milestones.blurb: On 15 September 1959, Doordarshan began experimental broadcasts from a makeshift Delhi studio — the flicker that would one day glow in half a billion homes.

milestones.headline: MAHARASHTRA AND GUJARAT ARE BORN
milestones.blurb: On 1 May 1960, Bombay State split into Maharashtra and Gujarat — two proud new states celebrated to this day as Maharashtra Day and Gujarat Day.

milestones.headline: GOA COMES HOME AFTER 451 YEARS
milestones.blurb: On 19 December 1961, Operation Vijay ended four and a half centuries of Portuguese rule in 36 hours, and Goa, Daman and Diu joined the Indian Union.

milestones.headline: GOLDEN GOAL: INDIA CONQUERS ASIAN FOOTBALL
milestones.blurb: On 4 September 1962, India beat South Korea 2-1 before 100,000 in Jakarta to win Asian Games football gold — the crowning glory of Indian football's golden era.

milestones.headline: INDIA'S SPACE DREAM LIFTS OFF THUMBA
milestones.blurb: On 21 November 1963, India's first rocket rose from a fishing village in Kerala — its parts carried to the launch pad by bicycle and bullock cart.

milestones.headline: HOCKEY CROWN RECLAIMED IN TOKYO
milestones.blurb: On 23 October 1964, India beat Pakistan 1-0 in the Tokyo Olympic final to reclaim hockey gold — the nation's seventh, and sweetest, Olympic hockey title.

milestones.headline: JAI JAWAN JAI KISAN RALLIES A NATION
milestones.blurb: In 1965, Prime Minister Lal Bahadur Shastri gave India its beloved rallying cry — 'Hail the soldier, hail the farmer' — saluting the two hands that fed and guarded the nation.

milestones.headline: INDIRA LEADS, REITA CONQUERS THE WORLD
milestones.blurb: On 24 January 1966, Indira Gandhi became India's first woman Prime Minister; on 17 November, medical student Reita Faria became the first Asian crowned Miss World.

milestones.headline: DR. ZAKIR HUSAIN RISES TO RASHTRAPATI BHAVAN
milestones.blurb: On 13 May 1967, scholar Zakir Husain became India's first Muslim President, weeks after the nation's fourth general election reaffirmed its young democracy.

milestones.headline: WHEAT REVOLUTION: INDIA'S FIELDS TURN GOLD
milestones.blurb: The Green Revolution delivered a record 16.5-million-tonne wheat harvest, celebrated with a special 'Wheat Revolution' stamp in July 1968 — the same year India won its first overseas Test series, in New Zealand.

milestones.headline: BANKS FOR THE PEOPLE, ROCKETS FOR TOMORROW
milestones.blurb: On 19 July 1969, fourteen major banks were nationalised to take credit to the masses; on 15 August, ISRO was founded under Vikram Sarabhai to take India to the stars.

milestones.headline: OPERATION FLOOD: WHITE REVOLUTION BEGINS
milestones.blurb: In 1970, Verghese Kurien launched Operation Flood, the world's largest dairy programme — village cooperatives that would make India the planet's biggest milk producer.

milestones.headline: VICTORY IN THE EAST: BANGLADESH IS BORN
milestones.blurb: On 16 December 1971, Pakistani forces surrendered in Dhaka after a 13-day war — India's most decisive military victory, midwifing the new nation of Bangladesh.

milestones.headline: INDIA VOWS TO PROTECT ITS WILD
milestones.blurb: In September 1972, the Wildlife (Protection) Act became law, throwing a shield around tigers, elephants and a million wild things — the foundation of India's conservation story.

milestones.headline: PROJECT TIGER ROARS TO LIFE
milestones.blurb: On 1 April 1973, Project Tiger launched at Jim Corbett National Park with nine reserves — a bold gamble that pulled the national animal back from the brink.

milestones.headline: THE BUDDHA SMILES AT POKHRAN
milestones.blurb: On 18 May 1974, India conducted its first nuclear test in the Rajasthan desert, code-named 'Smiling Buddha' — becoming the sixth nation to join the nuclear club.

milestones.headline: ARYABHATA IN ORBIT, SHOLAY IN THEATRES
milestones.blurb: On 19 April 1975, India's first satellite Aryabhata reached orbit; on 15 August, 'Sholay' opened in cinemas and rode into legend as Bollywood's greatest blockbuster.

milestones.headline: FIVE LAKH FARMERS MAKE A MOVIE
milestones.blurb: In 1976, half a million Gujarat dairy farmers chipped in two rupees each to fund Shyam Benegal's 'Manthan' — India's first crowdfunded film, and they arrived by truckload to watch it.

milestones.headline: DEMOCRACY SPEAKS: FIRST NON-CONGRESS GOVERNMENT
milestones.blurb: On 24 March 1977, Morarji Desai was sworn in at the head of the Janata government — the ballot box ended the Emergency and proved Indian democracy unbreakable.

milestones.headline: MIRACLE BABY DURGA MAKES HISTORY
milestones.blurb: On 3 October 1978, Durga (Kanupriya Agarwal) was born in Kolkata — India's first test-tube baby and only the world's second, just 70 days after Britain's Louise Brown.

milestones.headline: NOBEL PEACE PRIZE FOR MOTHER TERESA
milestones.blurb: On 17 October 1979, the Nobel Peace Prize was awarded to Mother Teresa of Calcutta for her work among the poorest of the poor — she asked that the banquet money feed them instead.

milestones.headline: ROHINI RISES: INDIA JOINS SPACE CLUB
milestones.blurb: On 18 July 1980, the SLV-3 rocket carried the Rohini satellite to orbit from Sriharikota, making India only the sixth nation to launch a satellite on its own rocket.

milestones.headline: SEVEN ENGINEERS, ONE DREAM: INFOSYS FOUNDED
milestones.blurb: On 2 July 1981, N.R. Narayana Murthy and six engineers founded Infosys with Rs 10,000 borrowed from his wife Sudha — seed money for India's software revolution.

milestones.headline: ASIAD '82 BEAMS INDIA INTO COLOUR
milestones.blurb: On 19 November 1982, the Asian Games opened in New Delhi with mascot Appu the elephant — and colour television arrived in Indian homes just in time to watch.

milestones.headline: KAPIL'S DEVILS LIFT THE WORLD CUP
milestones.blurb: On 25 June 1983, underdogs India stunned the mighty West Indies at Lord's to win the Cricket World Cup; on 14 December, the first Maruti 800 rolled out to put India on wheels.

milestones.headline: SAARE JAHAN SE ACHHA, SAYS INDIA'S SPACEMAN
milestones.blurb: On 3 April 1984, Rakesh Sharma became the first Indian in space aboard Soyuz T-11. Asked how India looked from orbit, he replied: 'Saare Jahan Se Achha.'

milestones.headline: WORLD CHAMPIONS AGAIN — AND AN AUDI
milestones.blurb: On 10 March 1985, India beat Pakistan in Melbourne to win the World Championship of Cricket unbeaten — and man of the series Ravi Shastri drove the Audi home with the team aboard.

milestones.headline: LORD'S FALLS TO KAPIL'S INDIA
milestones.blurb: On 10 June 1986, India won a Test match at Lord's for the first time, Kapil Dev sealing it with a six — the launchpad of a famous 2-0 series triumph in England.

milestones.headline: RAMAYAN BRINGS INDIA TO A STANDSTILL
milestones.blurb: From 25 January 1987, Ramanand Sagar's 'Ramayan' aired Sunday mornings on Doordarshan — streets emptied and an estimated 100 million viewers made it a television epic.

milestones.headline: ANAND, 18, IS INDIA'S FIRST GRANDMASTER
milestones.blurb: In 1988, eighteen-year-old Viswanathan Anand became India's first chess grandmaster in Coimbatore — the opening move of a five-time world champion and a chess superpower.

milestones.headline: A 16-YEAR-OLD NAMED SACHIN DEBUTS
milestones.blurb: On 15 November 1989, sixteen-year-old Sachin Tendulkar walked out for his first Test in Karachi — the opening chapter of cricket's greatest 24-year love story.

milestones.headline: BOY WONDER SACHIN'S FIRST TEST CENTURY
milestones.blurb: On 14 August 1990, a 17-year-old Sachin Tendulkar struck an unbeaten 119 at Old Trafford to save the Test — the first of a world-record 100 international hundreds.

milestones.headline: INDIA OPENS ITS ECONOMY TO THE WORLD
milestones.blurb: On 24 July 1991, Finance Minister Manmohan Singh's budget swept away the licence raj, declaring 'no power on earth can stop an idea whose time has come.'

milestones.headline: AN OSCAR FOR SATYAJIT RAY
milestones.blurb: On 30 March 1992, Satyajit Ray received an honorary Academy Award for lifetime achievement — accepting from his Calcutta hospital bed, the same year India gave him the Bharat Ratna.

milestones.headline: INDIAN SKIES OPEN: JET AIRWAYS TAKES OFF
milestones.blurb: On 5 May 1993, Jet Airways flew its first flight as economic reforms opened Indian skies to private airlines — flying was no longer only for the few.

milestones.headline: SUSHMITA AND AISHWARYA CONQUER THE WORLD
milestones.blurb: In a fairytale double, Sushmita Sen was crowned Miss Universe on 21 May 1994 and Aishwarya Rai Miss World on 19 November — both global crowns in one Indian year.

milestones.headline: INDIA DIALS INTO THE DIGITAL AGE
milestones.blurb: On 31 July 1995, Jyoti Basu made India's first mobile phone call to Sukh Ram; on 15 August, VSNL switched on public internet — the connected century had begun.

milestones.headline: PAES ENDS 44-YEAR OLYMPIC MEDAL WAIT
milestones.blurb: On 3 August 1996, Leander Paes battled to tennis bronze in Atlanta — India's first individual Olympic medal in 44 years, won on heart as much as skill.

milestones.headline: FIFTY YEARS FREE, BARRIERS STILL FALLING
milestones.blurb: India celebrated 50 years of independence on 15 August 1997, weeks after K.R. Narayanan was sworn in on 25 July as the first Dalit President of the republic.

milestones.headline: POKHRAN ROARS, AMARTYA SEN WINS NOBEL
milestones.blurb: On 11 May 1998, India declared itself a nuclear power with the Pokhran-II tests; on 14 October, Amartya Sen won the Nobel in Economics for his work on welfare and famine.

milestones.headline: INFOSYS RINGS THE NASDAQ BELL
milestones.blurb: On 11 March 1999, Infosys became the first Indian company listed on America's NASDAQ — Bangalore's software engineers announced India's IT century to Wall Street.

milestones.headline: ONE BILLION INDIANS — AND ONE CROREPATI SHOW
milestones.blurb: On 11 May 2000, baby Aastha Arora was welcomed as India's billionth citizen; on 3 July, Amitabh Bachchan asked his first 'Kaun Banega Crorepati' question — lock kiya jaye?

milestones.headline: LAGAAN HITS INDIA FOR SIX
milestones.blurb: On 15 June 2001, 'Lagaan' opened in cinemas — its village XI beat the British at cricket and batted all the way to an Academy Award nomination.

milestones.headline: MISSILE MAN BECOMES PRESIDENT, METRO ROLLS
milestones.blurb: On 25 July 2002, scientist A.P.J. Abdul Kalam became the People's President; on 24 December, the Delhi Metro carried its first passengers into a new urban age.

milestones.headline: ANJU LEAPS INTO WORLD HISTORY
milestones.blurb: On 30 August 2003, Anju Bobby George jumped 6.70 metres in Paris to win long jump bronze — the first world athletics championship medal ever for India.

milestones.headline: RATHORE SHOOTS INDIA'S FIRST INDIVIDUAL SILVER
milestones.blurb: On 17 August 2004, Army marksman Rajyavardhan Singh Rathore won double-trap silver in Athens — India's first individual Olympic silver, and a starting gun for a shooting boom.

milestones.headline: RIGHT TO INFORMATION HANDS POWER TO PEOPLE
milestones.blurb: On 12 October 2005, the Right to Information Act came into force — for ten rupees, any citizen could now ask the government to open its files.

milestones.headline: KIRAN DESAI WINS THE BOOKER PRIZE
milestones.blurb: On 10 October 2006, Kiran Desai won the Man Booker Prize for 'The Inheritance of Loss' at 35 — the youngest woman ever to take literature's famous prize.

milestones.headline: DHONI'S YOUNG INDIA WINS FIRST T20 CUP
milestones.blurb: On 24 September 2007, M.S. Dhoni's young side beat Pakistan in Johannesburg to win the first-ever T20 World Cup, the year Pratibha Patil became India's first woman President on 25 July.

milestones.headline: GOLD, GLITZ AND A SHOT AT THE MOON
milestones.blurb: In 2008, Abhinav Bindra shot India's first individual Olympic gold on 11 August, the IPL exploded onto the scene in April, and Chandrayaan-1 left for the Moon on 22 October.

milestones.headline: JAI HO! RAHMAN WINS TWO OSCARS
milestones.blurb: On 22 February 2009, A.R. Rahman won two Academy Awards in one night for 'Slumdog Millionaire' — and 'Jai Ho' became the whole world's victory chant.

milestones.headline: AADHAAR BEGINS, DELHI THROWS A GAMES
milestones.blurb: On 29 September 2010, villager Ranjana Sonawane received the first Aadhaar number, launching the world's largest ID project; days later Delhi hosted the Commonwealth Games, where India bagged 101 medals.
